reasoning: The NBER Business Cycle Dating Committee defines a recession as a significant decline in economic activity that is spread across the economy and that lasts more than a few months. Throughout 2023 and 2024, US real GDP growth was consistently positive (e.g., 4.9% in Q3 2023, 3.3% in Q4 2023, and above 1% every quarter in 2024, with full year 2024 growth at 2.8%). The NBER has not made any announcement designating a peak in 2023 or 2024, and given the strong macroeconomic data (steady GDP, positive employment numbers), it is virtually impossible that they will retroactively do so before the end of 2025. This matches the exceptionally low market sentiment (1% as of late Oct 2025).
